locations.name: Griffith Park
locations.address: 4730 Crystal Springs Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90027
locations.summary: 4,210-acre urban park with hiking trails, Griffith Observatory, and Hollywood Sign views
locations.description: Griffith Park is one of the largest urban parks in North America, offering extensive hiking trails, picnic areas, and stunning views of Los Angeles and the iconic Hollywood Sign. While the Griffith Observatory and planetarium shows require tickets, the park grounds, hiking trails, and many viewpoints are completely free to explore.
locations.special_notes: Free park access. Observatory free admission but timed reservations required. Parking fees apply ($4-20). Great Hollywood Sign viewing spots.

locations.name: The Getty Center
locations.address: 1200 Getty Center Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90049
locations.summary: World-class art museum with stunning architecture, gardens, and panoramic city views
locations.description: The Getty Center is a world-renowned art museum featuring European paintings, drawings, sculptures, decorative arts, and photographs from the Middle Ages to the present. The complex is famous for its stunning architecture, beautiful gardens, and panoramic views of Los Angeles. While parking costs money, admission to the museum and all exhibitions is completely free.
locations.special_notes: Free admission with timed reservations required. Parking $20 per car. Closed Mondays. Arrive early for best parking availability.

locations.name: Hollywood Walk of Fame
locations.address: Hollywood Blvd & Vine St, Los Angeles, CA 90028
locations.summary: Iconic sidewalk with celebrity stars, historic theaters, and Hollywood glamour
locations.description: The Hollywood Walk of Fame is a world-famous attraction featuring more than 2,600 brass stars embedded in the sidewalks along Hollywood Boulevard and Vine Street. The walk celebrates achievements in the entertainment industry, with stars honoring actors, musicians, directors, and other celebrities. The surrounding area includes historic theaters like the TCL Chinese Theatre and the Dolby Theatre.
locations.special_notes: Completely free to walk around. Star ceremonies happen regularly. Busy area - be aware of surroundings. Paid parking available in nearby lots.

locations.name: Santa Monica Pier
locations.address: 200 Santa Monica Pier, Santa Monica, CA 90401
locations.summary: Historic pier with carnival rides, Pacific Park amusement area, and beachfront views
locations.description: The Santa Monica Pier is a historic landmark dating back to 1909, featuring the Pacific Park amusement area with rides like the solar-powered Pacific Wheel Ferris wheel and West Coaster roller coaster. While individual rides cost money, walking on the pier, enjoying ocean views, and watching the sunset are completely free. The pier offers classic beach town atmosphere with street performers, fishing, and dining options.
locations.special_notes: Free to walk on pier. Individual ride tickets required for attractions. Parking fees in nearby lots. Beautiful sunset viewing spot.

locations.name: Venice Beach Boardwalk
locations.address: 1800 Ocean Front Walk, Los Angeles, CA 90291
locations.summary: Eclectic boardwalk with street performers, artists, skate park, and Venice Canals
locations.description: The Venice Beach Boardwalk is one of LA's most iconic and colorful destinations, featuring a diverse array of street performers, artists, vendors, and attractions. The area includes the famous Venice Skate Park, outdoor basketball courts, bodybuilding areas, and numerous shops and cafes. The adjacent Venice Canals offer a peaceful contrast with beautiful residential waterways. It's a celebration of LA's bohemian beach culture.
locations.special_notes: Free to explore. Very crowded on weekends and summer. Unique street performers and artists. Venice Canals nearby worth visiting.

locations.name: Runyon Canyon Park
locations.address: 2000 N Fuller Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90046
locations.summary: Popular hiking trail with Hollywood Sign views and celebrity sightings
locations.description: Runyon Canyon Park is one of LA's most famous hiking spots, offering scenic trails with panoramic views of Los Angeles and the Hollywood Sign. The park attracts fitness enthusiasts, dog walkers, and celebrities seeking exercise. Multiple trail options range from easy walks to challenging hikes, making it accessible to various fitness levels. The park is known for its social scene and stunning city views.
locations.special_notes: Free admission. Limited street parking. Very popular - arrive early. Dogs allowed on-leash. Bring water and sun protection.

locations.name: Olvera Street
locations.address: 845 N Alameda St, Los Angeles, CA 90012
locations.summary: Birthplace of Los Angeles with historic Mexican marketplace and cultural attractions
locations.description: Olvera Street is known as the birthplace of Los Angeles, offering a vibrant taste of old Mexico with historic buildings, traditional restaurants, and colorful shops. The street features the Avila Adobe, the oldest standing residence in Los Angeles, along with museums, cultural performances, and authentic Mexican cuisine. It's a free outdoor museum celebrating LA's Mexican heritage and founding.
locations.special_notes: Free street access, some museums may charge admission. Cultural events and celebrations throughout the year. Authentic Mexican dining experience.

locations.name: The Grove
locations.address: 189 The Grove Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90036
locations.summary: Outdoor shopping center with beautiful landscaping, fountain shows, and free trolley
locations.description: The Grove is an upscale outdoor shopping and entertainment center featuring beautiful architecture, landscaping, and a choreographed fountain show. While shopping and dining cost money, visitors can enjoy free strolling through the property, watching the fountain shows, riding the free trolley to The Original Farmers Market, and admiring the ambiance. The center features a dancing water fountain synchronized to music and seasonal decorations.
locations.special_notes: Free to walk around and enjoy atmosphere. Fountain shows run hourly. Free trolley connects to Farmers Market. Shopping and dining cost extra.

best free attractions Los Angeles California

data_quality_goals.coordinate_accuracy: Must be within Los Angeles County bounds
